title: Ce ascund liderii politici când comunică prost?
summary: The article criticizes President Nicușor Dan's poor communication during a confrontation with civil society protesters demanding he reject controversial prosecutor nominations proposed by PSD Justice Minister Radu Marinescu. His arrogant dismissal of journalists and civil society has fueled suspicions that he has become a prisoner of the PSD-dominated political system, echoing the failed presidency of Emil Constantinescu.
talking_points: Nicușor Dan confronted Declic protesters and arrogantly claimed to have 'a hundred times more information' than them, alienating his own voter base
The president's ambiguous positioning toward PSD and failure to reform intelligence services suggest he may be captive to the existing power system
Civil society and professional journalists had concrete evidence of problems in prosecutors' CVs, making Dan's dismissal of their concerns unjustified
Being an independent president without party infrastructure makes Dan easy to manipulate by established political networks and intelligence services
A suspected package deal for appointing prosecutors and intelligence chiefs appears to have been pre-negotiated between parties and the president
author: Rodica Culcer
